author: Her

capabilities.description: Open a real Chrome (stable channel) with a persistent profile that reuses the user's logins, and show it in the browser drawer. Automation is hidden (navigator.webdriver is false). Returns the current page.
capabilities.title: Open browser

capabilities.description: Read the current page: URL, title, visible text, and a numbered list of interactive elements ([index] tag: label). Use the [index] with browser.click / browser.type to act reliably without guessing CSS selectors. Read again after each action to see the updated page.
capabilities.title: Read current page

capabilities.description: Check whether the browser presents as a normal human Chrome: navigator.webdriver, headless UA, plugins, WebGL vendor, CPU cores, languages. Use when the user asks if the browser is detectable.
capabilities.title: Check anti-detection

capabilities.description: Navigate the browser to a URL. The drawer shows the live page and the reply includes the new title/URL.

url.description: Destination URL (https:// is added if missing).

capabilities.title: Navigate browser

capabilities.description: Click in the page. Prefer an element index from browser.read's numbered list; a CSS selector or x/y coordinates also work.

index.description: Index of an element from browser.read's interactive-elements list.

selector.description: CSS selector of the element to click.

x.description: Click x coordinate (with y) if no selector.

y.description: Click y coordinate (with x) if no selector.

capabilities.title: Click in browser

capabilities.description: Type into the page with a real keyboard. Target a field by element index (from browser.read), a CSS selector, or type into whatever is currently focused (give only text — first browser.click the field, e.g. by coordinate). For rich editors like X's compose box (contenteditable / role=textbox), click the box then type with real keys so React sees the input. Set enter=true to submit simple forms; for X, click the Post button instead. Examples: {index: 3, text: "hello", enter: true}; click then {text: "my tweet"}; {key: "Escape"}.

index.description: Index of the field element from browser.read's interactive-elements list.

key.description: A single key to press instead of typing text (e.g. Enter, Escape, ArrowDown, Tab).

selector.description: CSS selector of the field to fill.

capabilities.title: Type in browser

description: Drive the user's real Chrome from the conversation: a persistent profile reuses logins and automation is hard to detect. Open it, read the page, and navigate/click/type with approval.
name: Browser Control
systemPromptAddendum: The browser is a real Chrome stable window with the user's persistent profile, so logins are already there and automation is hidden (navigator.webdriver is false). Loop: browser.read returns the page text plus a numbered list of interactive elements; pick an [index] and call browser.click or browser.type with it; then browser.read again to see the result; repeat until the goal is met. Input uses a real mouse and real keyboard (system-level in 专用 mode), so it works on rich React editors. For a contenteditable / role=textbox box such as X's tweet composer: browser.click the box (by its [index], or by x/y coordinates from the screenshot), then browser.type with just text and NO selector/index — the input lands on the focused editor (in the everyday-Chrome extension it auto-targets the focused or first visible editable, so a stale index won't matter). To publish, browser.click the 发帖/Post button rather than pressing Enter. If a hardened editor still rejects the text in 日常 (extension) mode, tell the user that 专用 Chrome mode uses a real system keyboard and is more reliable for sites like X (it needs a one-time login in that profile). browser.read and browser.detect are free; navigate/click/type each require approval UNLESS the user turned on the drawer's 自动操作 toggle, in which case chain actions freely while narrating. Never log into new accounts or submit sensitive/payment forms without the user explicitly asking.
